item.title: Integration of genetic evidence to identify approved drug targets.
item.description: Genome medicine - Drugs targeting genes supported by human genetic evidence are more likely to succeed in clinical trials. While previous approaches have benchmarked individual methods such as genome-wide associatio...

item.title: A genetic variant of adenylate cyclase 7 associated with ulcerative colitis shows impaired function and G-protein-coupled receptor signaling.
item.description: Human genetics - A missense variant of adenylate cyclase 7 (AC7), p.Asp439Glu, has been significantly associated with ulcerative colitis (UC) in genome-wide association studies. Previous work suggested that this va...

item.title: FCGR2A promoter variant reveals shared genetic susceptibility between IBD and stroke.
item.description: Molecular and cellular biochemistry - While the epidemiological association between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and stroke is well-established, the shared genetic architecture underlying these diseases remains unclear. This study ...

item.title: The causal relationship between the palmitoylation gene ZDHHC13 and ulcerative colitis: A Mendelian randomization study implicating CD62L-monocyte AC.
item.description: Medicine - This study explores the potential mechanisms between palmitoylation and ulcerative colitis (UC) through Mendelian randomization, bioinformatics analysis combined with eQTL and protein quantitative ...

item.title: Shared inflammatory architecture and therapeutic tensions between psoriasis and Crohn's disease.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - Psoriasis and Crohn's disease are chronic immune-mediated inflammatory diseases affecting distinct barrier organs, yet epidemiological, genetic, transcriptomic, and therapeutic evidence supports pa...
